title: [FIRRTL] infer width (not, tail, dshl ?)
body:
The following FIRRTL program

```python
circuit top_mod :
  module top_mod :
    input inp_j: UInt<56>
    output tmp43: UInt
    output tmp47: SInt
    tmp43 <= not(inp_j)
    tmp47 <= dshl(SInt("o10266562"), tail(tmp43, 49))
```

Compiled with `firtool a_top_mod.fir --lower-to-hw --expand-whens --infer-widths --lowering-options=noAlwaysFF --verilog -o=a_top_mod_new.v` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
module top_mod(
  input  [55:0]  inp_j,
  output [55:0]  tmp43,
  output [149:0] tmp47);

  wire [55:0] _tmp43_output;	// a_top_mod.fir:2:3

  assign _tmp43_output = ~inp_j;	// a_top_mod.fir:6:{11,14}
  wire [55:0] _T = _tmp43_output;	// a_top_mod.fir:7:38
  assign tmp43 = _T;	// a_top_mod.fir:2:3
  assign tmp47 = 150'h216D72 << {143'h0, _T[6:0]};	// a_top_mod.fir:2:3, :7:{14,38}
endmodule

```

Compiled with `firrtl-1.5-SNAPSHOT` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
module top_mod(
  input  [55:0] inp_j,
  output [55:0] tmp43,
  output [21:0] tmp47
);
  wire [149:0] _GEN_1 = 150'sh216d72 << tmp43[6:0];
  assign tmp43 = ~inp_j;
  assign tmp47 = _GEN_1[21:0];
endmodule
```
